navDesc.dashboard: View platform status overview: KPIs, execution trends, alert statistics
navDesc.apis: API asset management and AI analysis, quality scores and documentation
navDesc.mockServices: Standalone mock services for third-party and internal dependencies, with rule-based responses and logs
navDesc.scenarios: Test scenario orchestration, supports multi-step business validation
navDesc.factory: Test data factory, manage reusable test datasets
navDesc.monitor: Scheduled health checks, auto-poll API health status and alerting
navDesc.executions: View all execution history: single, scenario, and monitor runs
navDesc.settings: Platform settings: projects, environments, global configuration
navDesc.importDiffs: HAR diff comparison, detect API changes and generate alerts
navDesc.knowledge: Knowledge base for testing experience, business data, and feedback
navDesc.generations: Review AI outputs, accept, partially accept, or discard generated content
navDesc.coverage: Test asset coverage analysis, heatmap of coverage by module and dimension
navDesc.adminUsers: System user management, admin access only

common.name: Name
common.domainFilterHint: Controls which domains' APIs are collected during capture and HAR import. Supports wildcard * (e.g. *.example.com). Whitelist non-empty: only matching domains pass. Blacklist: always reject. Whitelist empty: only blacklist applies.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Platform Status Overview
dashboard.desc_kpi_apis: Total APIs discovered in the project, categorized by analysis status
dashboard.desc_kpi_scenarios: Total test scenarios, including AI-generated and manually created
dashboard.desc_kpi_pass_rate: Pass percentage across all recent executions (single API, scenario, monitor)
dashboard.desc_sla_availability: Service Level Agreement (SLA) global availability, showing compliant API count

dashboard.desc_chart_trend: Trend chart of execution totals and pass rates over time buckets

dashboard.desc_top_failing: Top APIs with the most failures within the selected time window
dashboard.desc_sla_report: Detailed SLA availability compliance for each API
dashboard.desc_health_score: Composite health score based on success rate, latency, and other metrics
dashboard.desc_recent_audit: Audit log of user operations, recording key platform changes
dashboard.workbench_unanalysed_apis_desc: Newly imported or failed APIs should enter AI analysis to generate docs and assert suggestions.
dashboard.workbench_pending_generations_desc: AI generated content has not been written to DSL yet and needs human review.
dashboard.workbench_low_quality_desc: Business docs or valid assertions are missing; quality assets should be completed.
dashboard.workbench_no_scenario_desc: These APIs are not covered by business scenarios yet; generate or compose scenarios.
dashboard.workbench_no_monitor_desc: Core APIs have no scheduled monitoring yet; create monitors for them.
dashboard.workbench_recent_failed_desc: Recent failed executions need root-cause diagnosis and linked diff or reviewable fixes.
dashboard.workbench_recent_alerts_desc: Monitor alerts need impact confirmation and may require diagnosis and fixes.
dashboard.workbench_failed_ai_tasks_desc: Failed AI analysis jobs need retry or LLM configuration checks.
dashboard.workbench_failed_diagnoses_desc: Failure diagnosis jobs are in an error state and need retry or log inspection.
dashboard.ai_outputs_desc: Review accepted, partially accepted, discarded, and pending counts for docs, assertions, scenarios, data templates, and monitors.
dashboard.desc_ai_generated: Total AI-generated content, grouped by accepted/pending status
dashboard.desc_ai_acceptance: Overall AI content acceptance rate (including partial)
dashboard.desc_ai_pending: Number of AI-generated items awaiting human review
dashboard.desc_ai_dlq: Number of AI jobs in dead-letter queue due to failures
dashboard.desc_ai_tokens: Total tokens consumed by AI generation (input + output)

alert_channels.title: Alert Channels

intro.p1: ApiPulse is an all-in-one API quality assurance platform covering API asset management, AI-powered analysis, test scenario orchestration, mock services, data factory, health monitoring, and more—helping teams build efficient and reliable API testing systems.

capabilities.title: API Asset Management
capabilities.desc: Multi-source import (Swagger/Postman/HAR/mitmproxy/cURL), automatic request/response parsing, unified API catalog

capabilities.title: AI Analysis
capabilities.desc: LLM-powered intelligent analysis of requests/responses, auto-generate test assertions, API docs, and test scenarios

capabilities.title: Scenario Orchestration
capabilities.desc: DAG-based visual orchestration of multi-step test scenarios with parameter passing, conditional branching, and environment variables

capabilities.title: Mock Services
capabilities.desc: Standalone mock services with route rule matching, dynamic response templates, and call log tracing

capabilities.title: Data Factory
capabilities.desc: Test data template engine with built-in Faker methods for batch generating diverse test data sets

capabilities.title: Health Monitoring
capabilities.desc: Scheduled health checks with automatic API polling and multi-channel alerting (DingTalk/WeCom/Slack/Webhook)

intro.p2: The platform uses projects for data isolation and supports multi-language (Chinese/English), dark/light theme switching, AI shortcuts (Cmd/Ctrl+K), and other convenience features.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.desc: Platform-wide status overview with key KPIs, execution trends, and test coverage heatmaps.
tabs: Overview: Core business KPI cards (total APIs, scenarios, executions, alerts)
Workbench: Personal workspace with quick access to frequently used features
features: Project filtering: filter dashboard data by project, all metrics update in real time on project switch
Coverage heatmap: display asset coverage matrix by module × dimension (scenarios/mocks/data factory/monitoring)
Execution trends: line chart showing recent execution success/failure trends

apis.title: APIs
apis.desc: Centralized API asset management with search, filtering, batch operations, and multi-format import.
features: Multiple import methods: Swagger/OpenAPI, Postman, HAR, mitmproxy live capture, cURL paste
Batch operations: batch delete, batch export, batch AI analysis
List view: displays API method, path, tags, project, quality score, last updated time
Filter & search: search by method, path, tag, or project
Quick create: manually fill in request details to create a new API

apidetail.title: API Detail
apidetail.desc: Detailed view of a single API including request/response inspection, AI analysis, and document/assertion generation.
features: Request/response viewer: structured display of Headers, Query, Body (JSON/XML/Form)
AI analysis: automatically analyze API structure and generate quality scores and improvement suggestions
Doc generation: AI-powered automatic API documentation generation
Assertion generation: auto-generate various assertion types (status code, response body, headers, etc.)
Scenario generation: auto-generate multi-step test scenarios from the current API
Direct request sender: built-in request sender with real-time response viewing
Endpoint and parameter values auto-replaced with selected execution environment values
Auth config: supports None / Bearer Token / Basic Auth / API Key / OAuth 2.0 etc.; bindable to global environment variables

Assertion editor: visual assertion editor supporting status code, response body JSONPath, response header, and response time assertions; each assertion includes an operator (equals/contains/regex/greater-than/less-than...) and expected value; AI one-click assertion recommendation

Parameter references: reference upstream step extracted variables via {{step_N.response.body.path}} syntax; supports data factory template references {{factory.field}} and environment variables {{env.BASE_URL}}
Database query references: configure SQL query steps in scenarios, with results referenced by subsequent steps via {{sql_step_N.rows[0].column}} syntax

Pre/post scripts: execute custom Python/JavaScript scripts before request sending (pre-script) and after response received (post-script) for dynamic parameter construction, data encryption/decryption, result validation, etc.
Extract variables: extract variables from responses via JSONPath / XPath / Regex and store in context for subsequent step references; extraction results viewable in the 'Extracted Variables' panel

mock.title: Mock Services
mock.desc: Standalone mock service management for simulating third-party or internal dependency responses.
features: Route rules: support path prefix/exact matching and HTTP method filtering
Response config: custom status codes, response headers, body (supports template variables)
Call logs: record request info, matched rules, and response content for each mock call
Latency simulation: configurable response delay to simulate network conditions
Multi-environment: each mock service has an independent URL, supports multiple versions running in parallel

scenarios.title: Scenarios
scenarios.desc: Test scenario list and management page, supporting scenario creation, editing, execution, and result viewing.
features: Scenario list: displays scenario name, project, tags, step count, last execution status
DAG visual orchestration: drag-and-drop step orchestration with visual parameter passing and dependency display
Step editing: supports API calls, mock requests, data factory references, assertions, and more step types
Batch execution: supports parallel/serial execution strategies for steps within a scenario
Execution tracking: real-time progress, step-level input/output, and assertion results
Data references: pass response data or data-factory-generated data between steps via variable references

factory.title: Data Factory
factory.desc: Test data factory for managing reusable data templates and datasets.
features: Data templates: define data structure with configurable generation rules per field
Field editor: visually edit field name, type, and generation rules
Faker methods: 50+ built-in Faker methods (name, address, email, phone, date, etc.)
Dataset management: batch preview, export, and import of generated data
Template references: scenarios can directly reference data templates for runtime data generation

executions.title: Execution History
executions.desc: View all types of execution history including single API, scenario, and monitor runs.
features: Execution record list: displays execution type, target name, status, duration, trigger method, timestamp
Filtering: filter by type (API/Scenario/Monitor), status (Success/Failed/Running), time range
Execution detail: view per-step request/response details, assertion results, and error messages
AI diagnosis: on execution failure, AI automatically analyzes the cause and suggests fixes
Result comparison: compare two execution results to identify regressions

monitor.title: Health Monitor
monitor.desc: Scheduled health check monitoring with automatic API polling and multi-channel alerting on anomalies.
features: Monitor tasks: define monitoring targets (scenario/API), execution frequency (cron expression), timeout
Scheduled execution: cron-based automatic triggering with manual on-demand execution
Alert management: view alert records, details, and recovery times
Multi-channel notification: DingTalk bot, WeCom, Slack Webhook, custom webhook
Health trends: display API availability and response time trends over time periods

generations.title: Review Center
generations.desc: Centralized review for AI-generated content—accept, partially accept, or discard AI outputs.
features: Generation list: view all AI outputs by type (doc/assertion/scenario/evaluation)
Review actions: accept (replace existing), partially accept (merge selected items), discard
Batch review: support batch approve or reject AI-generated results
Diff preview: preview the difference between old and new content before accepting

importdiffs.title: Diff Comparison
importdiffs.desc: Diff detection page for external format imports (HAR, etc.), used to discover API changes and decide whether to sync updates.
features: Change detection: auto-compare with existing APIs on import to discover path, method, request params, and response structure changes
Field-level diff: side-by-side comparison of Header, Query, Body fields with highlights for additions/deletions/modifications
Sync decision: choose 'Accept Change' or 'Keep Old' per field, with support for accept-all/discard-all
Change history: every diff comparison and decision is recorded for full API evolution traceability
Batch processing: preview diffs by API in batch and sync all with one click

knowledge.title: Knowledge Base
knowledge.desc: Knowledge accumulation and management system for storing testing experience, business data, and user feedback.
features: Knowledge entries: CRUD for knowledge fragments with tag-based categorization
Batch extraction: batch extract knowledge points from API responses
Consolidation: auto-merge similar entries to avoid knowledge redundancy
Knowledge search: search entries by keyword or tag to quickly locate needed content
Association references: knowledge entries can be linked to APIs/scenarios and referenced during scenario execution

coverage.title: Coverage
coverage.desc: Test asset coverage analysis tool displaying coverage of various dimensions (scenarios, mocks, data factory, monitoring, etc.) over APIs as a heatmap by module × dimension.
features: Heatmap view: display coverage matrix by module (row) × dimension (column) with color intensity indicating coverage ratio
Dimension filter: filter by scenario/mock/data factory/monitoring dimensions individually
Coverage stats: display coverage percentages and uncovered API counts per module and dimension
Drill-down analysis: click a heatmap cell to view the detailed list of uncovered APIs and quickly jump to create

settings.title: Settings
settings.desc: Platform-wide configuration page with multiple functional tabs.
features: LLM Config: configure AI model provider, API key, model name, and parameters
AI Tasks: manage AI-related background async tasks (analysis, generation, diagnosis, etc.)
Prompt Management: customize AI prompt templates per task type
Dead Letter Queue: view and manage failed messages/tasks
Environments: manage multiple execution environments (dev/test/staging/prod), configure base URLs, variables, etc.
General: platform-wide preference settings
Projects: create, edit, delete projects, configure domain allow/block list filtering
Alert Channels: manage DingTalk/WeCom/Slack/Webhook notification channels

users.title: User Management (Admin)
users.desc: System user management page, accessible only to admin role users. Supports user creation, editing, deletion, and role assignment.
features: User list: displays username, email, role, creation time, last login time
Role management: supports Admin and User roles; Admin has full permissions
Create/edit users: admin can create new users or edit existing user info (username, email, password, role)
Delete/disable: supports deleting or disabling user accounts; disabled users cannot log in

list.title: From Traffic Capture to API Analysis
list.background: Use mitmproxy to capture client network traffic, or export HAR files from the browser, to quickly import external requests as API assets and leverage AI for deep analysis.
list.flow: mitmproxy live capture / browser HAR export → paste into API import → system auto-parses and creates API assets → go to API detail → click AI analyze → get quality scores and suggestions → generate assertions and API docs
tips: mitmproxy is ideal for live capture of mobile/web requests; HAR is suitable for batch browser traffic import
Import auto-deduplicates; existing APIs go to diff comparison instead of duplicate creation

list.title: Create and Execute Test Scenarios
list.background: Orchestrate multiple APIs into test scenarios following business flows, set up parameter passing and assertions, and execute with one click.
list.flow: Go to Scenarios → create new → set name and tags → add steps in DAG orchestrator (API call/assertion/delay etc.) → configure parameter passing between steps → save → click execute → view real-time progress and step-level results
tips: Select the execution environment at the bottom before running
Drag step nodes to adjust execution order
Reference upstream data with {{step_X.response.body.field}} syntax

list.title: Generate Test Data with Data Factory
list.background: When test scenarios require diverse test data, use the data factory to batch-generate realistic datasets.
list.flow: Go to Data Factory → create new template → define fields (name/email/phone/date etc.) → select Faker method per field → preview generated data → save template → reference template in scenario steps → data auto-generated at runtime
tips: Supports batch generation (1-1000 records), exportable as JSON/CSV
Fields support three modes: fixed value, Faker method, and regex pattern

list.title: Scheduled Health Monitoring
list.background: Set up scheduled monitoring tasks for critical APIs or scenarios to automatically check health status and alert on anomalies.
list.flow: Ensure target API/scenario is ready → go to Monitor → create new task → select target (scenario/API) → set cron frequency → configure alert channels → enable task → system runs on schedule → auto-send alerts on anomalies
tips: Cron expressions use five-field format (min hour day month weekday)
Alert channels must be configured in Settings first
Real-time alert notifications via WebSocket

list.title: AI-Assisted Full Workflow
list.background: Leverage AI throughout the entire workflow from API import to scenario execution for efficiency gains.
list.flow: Import API → AI analyzes structure and quality → AI generates assertions → AI generates API docs → AI generates test scenarios → review AI output in Review Center → accept content → execute scenarios → AI auto-diagnoses on failure
tips: AI-generated content requires human review; the Review Center provides a unified review entry point
Use Cmd/Ctrl+K anywhere to invoke the AI assistant

list.title: Mock Third-Party Dependencies
list.background: When the system under test depends on third-party services that are not yet available, use Mock services to simulate their responses.
list.flow: Go to Mock Services → create new service → add route rules (path, HTTP method) → configure response template (status, body, headers) → enable service → get mock URL → use mock URL in scenario steps or direct requests instead of real endpoints
tips: Mock responses support template variables that can reference request parameters for dynamic responses
Call logs help debug whether mock rules are matching as expected

list.title: Diff Comparison & API Sync
list.background: When existing APIs are re-imported (e.g., HAR/Swagger updates), use the diff comparison feature to compare old and new versions and selectively sync changes.
list.flow: Import HAR/Swagger etc. → system detects existing APIs → enter Diff Comparison page → view side-by-side field-level comparison (additions/deletions/modifications highlighted) → decide per field to accept or keep → submit sync → API assets updated per decisions
tips: Diff Comparison page groups by API; each API has independent decisions
Support 'Accept All' for quick full sync, 'Discard All' to keep current versions
Diff records are traceable for auditing API change history

list.title: Cross-Environment API Regression Testing
list.background: Run the same test scenario across multiple execution environments (dev/test/staging/prod) and compare results to identify environment-specific differences or regressions.
list.flow: Ensure all environments configured in Settings → go to Scenarios → select a scenario → switch target environment → execute separately → go to Execution History → compare two execution results → identify environment differences or regressions → fix and re-validate
tips: Execution environments are pre-configured in Settings → Environments with Base URLs and variables
After executing the same scenario across environments, use 'Result Comparison' in Execution History for step-by-step diff
Add regression scenarios to Monitor tasks for scheduled automated regression testing

items.title: Variable Reference Syntax
items.desc: In any text field of a scenario step (URL, Header, Body, etc.), use the {{ }} double curly brace syntax to reference context variables, which are automatically replaced with actual values at runtime.

samples.label: Reference upstream step response body field
samples.code: {{step_1.response.body.data.id}}

samples.label: Reference upstream step response header
samples.code: {{step_1.response.headers.Authorization}}

samples.label: Reference upstream step status code
samples.code: {{step_1.response.status}}

samples.label: Reference environment variable
samples.code: {{env.BASE_URL}}/api/users

samples.label: Reference data factory template field
samples.code: {{factory.user_template.email}}

samples.label: Reference database query result
samples.code: {{sql_step_2.rows[0].user_id}}

samples.label: Nested reference (field contains variable)
samples.code: {"user_id": {{step_1.response.body.id}}}

items.title: Assertion Configuration Examples
items.desc: Assertions are used to verify whether API responses meet expectations. Each assertion includes: assertion type, operator, and expected value.

samples.label: Status code assertion
samples.code: Type: Status Code → Operator: equals → Expected: 200

samples.label: JSONPath assertion
samples.code: Type: JSONPath → Expression: $.data.name → Operator: equals → Expected: "John"

samples.label: Response header assertion
samples.code: Type: Header → Header: Content-Type → Operator: contains → Expected: application/json

samples.label: Response time assertion
samples.code: Type: Response Time → Operator: less than → Expected: 2000 (ms)

samples.label: Regex assertion
samples.code: Type: Body Regex → Operator: matches → Expected: \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}

samples.label: Array length assertion
samples.code: Type: JSONPath → Expression: $.data[*] → Operator: greater than → Expected: 0

items.title: Authentication Configuration Examples
items.desc: Configure authentication in the 'Auth' tab of the API detail page, supporting multiple mainstream auth types.

samples.label: Bearer Token
samples.code: Type: Bearer Token → Token: {{env.API_TOKEN}} or enter token value directly

samples.label: Basic Auth
samples.code: Type: Basic Auth → Username: admin → Password: {{env.BASIC_PASS}}

samples.label: API Key
samples.code: Type: API Key → Key: X-API-Key → Value: {{env.API_KEY}} → Location: Header

samples.label: OAuth 2.0
samples.code: Type: OAuth 2.0 → Grant Type: Client Credentials → Token URL: {{env.AUTH_URL}}/token → Client ID: {{env.CLIENT_ID}}

samples.label: No Auth
samples.code: Type: None → No authentication information attached

items.title: Mock Response Template Examples
items.desc: Mock service response bodies support template variables, which can reference request parameters to dynamically generate response content.

samples.label: Reference request path parameter
samples.code: {"user_id": {{request.params.userId}}, "name": "mock_user_{{request.params.userId}}"}

samples.label: Reference request body field
samples.code: {"echo": {{request.body.message}}, "timestamp": {{request.body.ts}}

samples.label: Fixed JSON response
samples.code: {"code": 0, "message": "success", "data": {"id": 1, "name": "test"}}

samples.label: Random data response
samples.code: {"code": 0, "data": {"order_no": "ORD{{$randomInt(1000,9999)}}", "time": "{{$isoTimestamp}}"}}

items.title: Data Factory Field Configuration Examples
items.desc: Each field in a data factory template supports three generation modes: fixed value, Faker method, and regular expression.

samples.label: Fixed value
samples.code: Field: status → Mode: Fixed → Value: "active"

samples.label: Faker - Name
samples.code: Field: name → Mode: Faker → Method: name.findName() → Generates: "John"

samples.label: Faker - Email
samples.code: Field: email → Mode: Faker → Method: internet.email() → Generates: "test@example.com"

samples.label: Faker - Phone
samples.code: Field: phone → Mode: Faker → Method: phone.phoneNumber() → Generates: "13812345678"

samples.label: Faker - Date
samples.code: Field: created_at → Mode: Faker → Method: date.past() → Generates: "2024-03-15"

samples.label: Regular expression
samples.code: Field: order_id → Mode: Regex → Expression: ORD-[A-Z0-9]{8} → Generates: "ORD-XK9M2B7F"

items.title: Monitor Cron Expression Examples
items.desc: Monitor tasks use five-field Cron expressions (minute hour day month weekday) to set execution frequency.

samples.label: Every hour
samples.code: 0 * * * * (on the hour)

theme.title: Theme Switching
theme.desc: The platform supports dark and light themes. The theme toggle at the bottom of the sidebar switches with one click. Preferences are saved to local storage and automatically applied on next visit.

lang.title: Language Switching
lang.desc: The platform supports Chinese and English UI languages. To switch: Settings → General → Language selection, or specify via URL parameter. Changes take effect immediately without a page refresh.

ai.title: AI Shortcut
ai.desc: Press Cmd+K (macOS) or Ctrl+K (Windows/Linux) on any page to invoke the global AI assistant panel. The assistant can answer questions, explain concepts, and assist with troubleshooting. LLM provider and API key must be configured in Settings first.

project.title: Project Selection & Data Isolation
project.desc: The platform uses projects for data isolation. The project selector at the bottom of the sidebar switches the active project. APIs, scenarios, mock services, and execution records across different projects are fully isolated. Domain filtering can further filter API assets within each project by domain allow/block list.

roles.title: Roles & Permissions
roles.desc: The platform has two built-in roles: Admin and User. Admin has full permissions including user management, system settings, project management, and other sensitive operations. User can perform daily business operations (API management, scenario execution, mock configuration, etc.) but cannot access user management and certain system settings. Newly registered users default to the User role and require an Admin to upgrade permissions in User Management.
